name: voice
context: Make and receive programmable voice calls with Twilio and TwiML
info: This skill covers making outbound voice calls, receiving inbound calls via webhooks, controlling call flow with TwiML, recording calls, and building conference bridges. The primary interface is `client.calls.create()` in the Twilio Node.js helper library. Outbound calls connect a Twilio phone number to a destination and execute TwiML instructions (inline or from a URL). Inbound calls trigger a webhook where your server responds with TwiML to say text, play audio, gather DTMF input, record, or transfer. Status callbacks track call lifecycle events.

sections.title: Outbound calls
sections.body: Use `client.calls.create({ to, from, twiml })` for inline TwiML or `{ to, from, url }` for dynamic TwiML from a server. The `to` and `from` must be E.164 format. The response includes `callSid`, `status` (initially `queued`), and `direction`. See [[twilio#phone-numbers]] for number requirements.

sections.title: TwiML response
sections.body: Incoming call webhooks must return TwiML XML. Use the `VoiceResponse` class from the Twilio helper library to build TwiML programmatically. Key verbs: `<Say>` for text-to-speech, `<Play>` for audio, `<Gather>` for DTMF input, `<Record>` for recording, `<Dial>` for transferring or conferencing. See [[twilio/twiml#voice-verbs]] for the full verb reference.

sections.title: Call recording
sections.body: Record calls with the `<Record>` verb or by setting `record: true` on `client.calls.create()`. Recordings are stored in your Twilio account and accessible via the Recordings API. Enable `transcribe: true` for automatic speech-to-text transcription. Recordings can be downloaded as WAV or MP3.

sections.title: Conference calls
sections.body: Use `<Dial><Conference>room-name</Conference></Dial>` to connect callers into a shared room. Options include `startConferenceOnEnter`, `endConferenceOnExit`, `muted`, `beep`, and `maxParticipants`. Moderator controls are available via the Conference Participant API. See [[twilio/twiml#voice-verbs]] for conference TwiML.

sections.title: Status callbacks
sections.body: Set `statusCallback` on call creation to receive lifecycle events. Twilio POSTs `CallSid`, `CallStatus`, `CallDuration`, `Timestamp`, and `Direction`. Statuses progress: `initiated` > `ringing` > `answered` > `completed`. Error states include `busy`, `no-answer`, `canceled`, and `failed` with `ErrorCode`.

scripts.name: Make Call
scripts.description: Place an outbound voice call using the Twilio Programmable Voice API

TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER.description: Twilio phone number to call from (E.164 format)

to.description: Destination phone number in E.164 format

from.description: Twilio phone number to call from (E.164 format)

twiml.description: Inline TwiML XML to execute when the call connects

url.description: URL that returns TwiML for dynamic call control

statusCallback.description: URL to receive call status updates

callSid.description: Unique identifier for the call (starts with CA)

status.description: Call status (queued, ringing, answered, completed, failed)

duration.description: Call duration in seconds (available after completion)

depends.reason: Requires Twilio account credentials and a phone number
TwiML controls the call flow for both outbound and inbound calls
